summary: McKenzie briefly recounts having so many domain names that a bulk renewal triggered a fraud alert from his credit card company. He spent $600 at GoDaddy for multi-year renewals across dozens of domains, plus $160 for a buyer's club membership to get discounts. The post is a lighthearted aside about the hidden costs of being a serial project starter who accumulates domains for various business ideas.
key_points: Bulk domain renewals can trigger credit card fraud alerts, illustrating how quickly costs accumulate
GoDaddy's buyer's club membership can pay for itself if you own enough domains, particularly .net domains at 53% off
Owning many domains without developed sites creates a backlog that requires outside help to address
money_quotes: you have to phone Chase in the middle of the night to say the $600 charge to GoDaddy was authorized and it was a very good deal, too, thank you very much.
A few dozen domains times two year renewal adds up really, really quickly…
I really need to get some freelancers to help me fill these things out.
key_insight: The entrepreneurial habit of registering domains for every business idea quietly accumulates into a surprisingly expensive portfolio of unrealized projects.
